card.intuition: Techniques for handling massive amounts of data and traffic that a single database instance cannot manage alone: Replication, Sharding, and Federation.
card.analogy: A library: **Replication** is making 5 copies of a popular book so 5 people can read it at once. **Sharding** is splitting the library by letter (A-M in building 1, N-Z in building 2). **Federation** is having separate libraries for Comics vs Textbooks.
when_to_use: Replication: When you have 10,000 'Reads' (Views) but only 10 'Writes' (Posts)
Sharding: When your data is so huge it won't fit on one physical hard drive
Caching: Using Redis to store frequent queries and take pressure off the main DB
gotchas: Replication Lag: if you write to the Master and immediately try to read from a Replica, the data might not be there yet! (Eventual Consistency).
Sharding Complexity: once you shard, you can no longer do 'JOINS' between shards easily. You have to do them in your application code.

exercise.prompt: Identify the scaling pattern that involves splitting a single large dataset into smaller pieces across multiple servers.
